prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French: Le silence entre nous disait plus que n'aurait pu le faire la plus grande des disputes.
Note: "More than any argument could have" is rendered as "plus que n'aurait pu le faire la plus grande des disputes" to emphasize the depth and intensity of the silence compared to even the most heated argument.

Japanese: 私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな喧嘩よりも多くを物語っていた。
(Watashitachi no aida no chinmoku wa, dōyatte no kenka yori mo ōku o monogatatte ita.)
Note: The phrase "said more" is translated as "多くを物語っていた" (ōku o monogatatte ita), which literally means "told a lot" or "spoke volumes," to convey the idea that the silence communicated more than words could.

Arabic: الصمت بيننا كان يتحدث أكثر مما كان يمكن لأي خلاف أن يفعل.
(Aṣ-ṣamt baynā kāna yaḥaddithu akthara mimā kāna yumkin li'ay khilāf an yaʿmal.)
Note: The phrase "could have" is rendered as "كان يمكن" (kāna yumkin), which literally means "could" or "was possible," to express the hypothetical nature of what an argument might have said in comparison to the silence.
